title: ollama/ollama
summary: Official Ollama repo: get up and running with Llama 2, Mistral, Gemma, and other large language models locally.

title: ollama-python
summary: Official Python SDK for Ollama; chat, embed, pull, and manage models with a few lines of code.

title: ollama-js
summary: Official JavaScript/TypeScript client for Ollama; works in Node and modern browsers.

title: langchain-ollama
summary: LangChain adapter to use any Ollama model as an LLM or chat component inside LangChain pipelines.

title: ollama-webui
summary: Feature-rich chat WebUI for Ollama (now renamed “Open WebUI”); install via Docker in one command.

title: ollama4j
summary: Java/Kotlin client for Ollama; sync/async APIs, fluent builder style, Android compatible.

title: ollama-rb
summary: Official Ruby gem for Ollama; simple interface to chat, generate, embed, and pull models.

title: ollama-cli
summary: Community-built interactive CLI with conversation history, syntax highlighting, and prompt templates.

title: ollama-copilot
summary: VS Code extension that turns any Ollama model into a local GitHub Copilot alternative.

title: ollama-haystack
summary: Haystack integration to use Ollama models as generators or embedders in Haystack 2.0 pipelines.

title: ollama-csharp
summary: Official C#/.NET SDK for Ollama; supports .NET 6+ and Unity via REST client.

title: ollama-discord
summary: Self-host Discord bot that brings Ollama models into any server with slash commands.

title: ollama-helm
summary: Production-ready Helm chart to deploy Ollama on Kubernetes with GPU autoscaling.

title: ollama-slack
summary: Lightweight Slack bot that streams Ollama replies into threads; deploy via one Docker command.

title: ollama-dagger
summary: Dagger module that caches and serves Ollama models inside CI pipelines for reproducible LLM tests.

title: ollama-rust
summary: Community-maintained Rust crate providing strongly-typed async bindings to the Ollama API.

title: ollama-streamlit
summary: Minimal Streamlit chat UI that auto-detects local Ollama models and supports file uploads for RAG.
